Importing and installing libraries¶

In [ ]:
from importlib.metadata import version
In [ ]:
pip install mediacloud
In [ ]:
pip install python-dotenv
In [ ]:
pip install pandas
In [ ]:
pip install seaborn
In [ ]:
pip install wordcloud
In [ ]:
pip install spacy
In [2]:
import pandas as pd   #data cleaning and wrangling
import mediacloud.api #data collection
import inspect        #understanding poorly documented APIs/libraries

from datetime import datetime        #managing publication dates
from calendar import monthrange      #working with time loops and monthly queries (data collection)
import time                          #handling with APIs rate time limits
from datetime import date, timedelta #temporal operations
import os                            #operating with the operating system
import pprint                        #print data structure in a readable way (e.g., API responses)
from pathlib import Path             #more sofisticated path/file management
import glob                          #look for file matching on the basis of a pattern

import seaborn as sns              #visualizing coverage trends
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt    #base library to plot
import matplotlib.dates as mdates  #specialized module for time series
import re                          #regex

 Personal API key¶

In [3]:
API_key = "8e6e80ca06afa15cd5f58fab33f6dc5d457fe2da"
In [4]:
#Creating the client

mc = mediacloud.api.SearchApi(API_key)

print(inspect.signature(mc.story_list))

(query: str, start_date: datetime.date, end_date: datetime.date, collection_ids: Optional[List[int]] = [], source_ids: Optional[List[int]] = [], platform: Optional[str] = None, expanded: Optional[bool] = None, pagination_token: Optional[str] = None, sort_order: Optional[str] = None, page_size: Optional[int] = None) -> tuple[typing.List[typing.Dict], typing.Optional[str]]
In [ ]:
#Creating a Client for API directory

directory_api = mediacloud.api.DirectoryApi(API_key)
f'Using Media Cloud python client v{version("mediacloud")}'
In [ ]:
# search for a collection by name

italian_collections = directory_api.collection_list(name='italy')
[c['name'] for c in italian_collections['results']]
In [ ]:
# page through a list of all the sources in the "Italy - National" collection
ITALY_NATIONAL = 34412372
sources_collection = []
limit = 100
offset = 0
while True:
    response = directory_api.source_list(collection_id=ITALY_NATIONAL, limit=limit, offset=offset)
    sources_collection += response['results']
    if response['next'] is None:
        break
    offset += limit
f"Found {len(sources_collection)} media sources in Italy National collection geographic collections"
In [ ]:
print(type(sources_collection))
for key in sources_collection[0].keys():
    print(key)
In [ ]:
pprint.pprint(sources_collection[0])
In [ ]:
df_sources = pd.json_normalize(sources_collection)
print(df_sources.columns)
df_sources[["id","name","label","media_type","platform"]]

Data collection¶

News data were collected through the SearchAPI service for three Italian newspaper outlets selected according to ADS readership data for 2017. The sample was constructed to capture variation in both journalistic style and political orientation.

Selected outlets:

  • La Repubblica (ID: 41552) — quality/progressive newspaper
  • Corriere della Sera (ID: 39529) — quality/conservative newspaper
  • Libero (ID: 308994) — right-wing populist tabloid

Observation period: January–December 2016.

Reference year: 2016

Metadata¶

In [5]:
START = date(2016, 1, 1)
END = date(2016, 12, 31)

sources = {
    41552: "La Repubblica",
    39529: "Il Corriere della Sera",
    308994: "Libero"
}

rows = []

for source_id, source_name in sources.items():
    results = mc.story_count_over_time(
        "*",
        START,
        END,
        source_ids=[source_id]
    )

    for r in results:
        rows.append({
            "source_id": source_id,
            "media_name": source_name,
            "date": r["date"],
            "total_stories": r["count"],
        })

denominator1 = pd.DataFrame(rows)
denominator1 = denominator1.rename(columns={"date": "publish_date"})
In [6]:
START = date(2017, 6, 11)
END = date(2018, 2, 19)

sources = {
    41552: "La Repubblica",
    39529: "Il Corriere della Sera",
    308994: "Libero"
}

rows = []

for source_id, source_name in sources.items():
    results = mc.story_count_over_time(
        "*",
        START,
        END,
        source_ids=[source_id]
    )

    for r in results:
        rows.append({
            "source_id": source_id,
            "media_name": source_name,
            "date": r["date"],
            "total_stories": r["count"],
        })

denominator2 = pd.DataFrame(rows)
denominator2 = denominator2.rename(columns={"date": "publish_date"})
In [7]:
# Merging datasets

denominator = pd.concat(
    [denominator1, denominator2],
    axis=0,
    ignore_index=True
)
denominator.head()
Out[7]:
source_id media_name publish_date total_stories
0 41552 La Repubblica 2016-01-01 120
1 41552 La Repubblica 2016-01-02 167
2 41552 La Repubblica 2016-01-03 139
3 41552 La Repubblica 2016-01-04 207
4 41552 La Repubblica 2016-01-05 236
In [8]:
denominator["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(denominator["publish_date"])

denominator["full_2016"] = (
    (denominator["publish_date"] >= "2016-01-01") &
    (denominator["publish_date"] <= "2016-12-31")
).astype(int)

denominator["pre_interview"] = (
    (denominator["publish_date"] >= "2017-06-11") &
    (denominator["publish_date"] <= "2017-11-19")
).astype(int)

denominator["post_interview"] = (
    (denominator["publish_date"] >= "2017-09-11") &
    (denominator["publish_date"] <= "2018-02-19")
).astype(int)

denominator.head()
Out[8]:
source_id media_name publish_date total_stories full_2016 pre_interview post_interview
0 41552 La Repubblica 2016-01-01 120 1 0 0
1 41552 La Repubblica 2016-01-02 167 1 0 0
2 41552 La Repubblica 2016-01-03 139 1 0 0
3 41552 La Repubblica 2016-01-04 207 1 0 0
4 41552 La Repubblica 2016-01-05 236 1 0 0
In [9]:
denominator_2016 = denominator[denominator["full_2016"] == 1].copy()
denominator_2016["year"] = denominator_2016["publish_date"].dt.year

country_yearly_total = (
    denominator_2016
    .groupby("year", as_index=False)["total_stories"]
    .sum()
    .rename(columns={"total_stories": "country_total_stories_2016"})
)

media_yearly_total = (
    denominator_2016
    .groupby(["source_id", "media_name", "year"], as_index=False)["total_stories"]
    .sum()
    .rename(columns={"total_stories": "media_total_stories_2016"})
)
In [10]:
#Saving the dataset
country_yearly_total.to_csv("ITA_country_total.csv", index=False, encoding="utf-8")
media_yearly_total.to_csv("ITA_media_total.csv", index=False, encoding="utf-8")
In [11]:
#Grouping news per month

denominator_int = denominator[(denominator["pre_interview"] == 1) | (denominator["post_interview"] == 1)].copy()
denominator_int["month"] = denominator_int["publish_date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)
denominator_int.head()
Out[11]:
source_id media_name publish_date total_stories full_2016 pre_interview post_interview month
1059 41552 La Repubblica 2017-06-11 164 0 1 0 2017-06
1060 41552 La Repubblica 2017-06-12 242 0 1 0 2017-06
1061 41552 La Repubblica 2017-06-13 290 0 1 0 2017-06
1062 41552 La Repubblica 2017-06-14 276 0 1 0 2017-06
1063 41552 La Repubblica 2017-06-15 271 0 1 0 2017-06
In [12]:
country_monthly_total = (
    denominator_int
    .groupby("month", as_index=False)["total_stories"]
    .sum()
    .rename(columns={"total_stories": "country_total_stories_month"})
)

media_monthly_total = (
    denominator_int
    .groupby(["source_id", "media_name", "month"], as_index=False)["total_stories"]
    .sum()
    .rename(columns={"total_stories": "media_total_stories_month"})
)
In [13]:
#Saving the dataset
country_monthly_total.to_csv("ITA_country_month.csv", index=False, encoding="utf-8")
media_monthly_total.to_csv("ITA_media_month.csv", index=False, encoding="utf-8")

Data wrangling¶

The data cleaning procedure involved the following steps:

  • Removal of non-informative variables: indexed_date, media_url, url
  • Cleaning up of cases that may be source of noise: language [=it], only title including words related to migration, removal of duplicates
  • Reconding of variables in a more readable way: media_name
In [14]:
#Opening the file

df_imm_2016 = pd.read_csv("IT_2016_stories.csv")
df_imm_2016.head()

df_imm_pre = pd.read_csv("IT_pre_stories.csv")

df_imm_post = pd.read_csv("IT_post_stories.csv")
df_imm_post.head()

df_imm_int = pd.read_csv("IT_int_stories.csv")

descriptives_date = (
    df_imm
    .groupby("media_name")
    .agg(
        min_date=("publish_date", "min"),
        max_date=("publish_date", "max"),
        n_stories=("publish_date", "count")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

df_imm["media_categories"] = (
    df_imm["media_name"].map(outlet_categories)
)

TITLE: Bologna e Impact, nasce la Coppa Saputo per le scuole di città e provincia

ARTICLE 5
TITLE: Roma, in piazza il posto auto riservato del presidente M5s del municipio III

ARTICLE 6
TITLE: Moscovici all'Eurogruppo: "Disponibili con l'Italia, ma sia seria sul deficit"

ARTICLE 7
TITLE: Selvaggia lite tra due amici ubriachi a Ivrea: uno finisce in ospedale, l'altro in prigione

ARTICLE 8
TITLE: Anti-Trump e pro-Palestina Roger Waters chiude il Desert Trip

ARTICLE 9
TITLE: Impiegati da tutto il mondo «Babele? In Posta funziona»

ARTICLE 10
TITLE: I carabinieri arrestano due rom accusati di sequestro di persona

Cleaning up for title¶

Dictionary-based filtering of migration-related news articles using a rule-based textual classification approach.

The regex pattern is constructed as:

  • a raw string (r"...")
  • bounded by word boundaries (\b) to avoid partial-word matches
  • a non-capturing group (?:...) containing alternative migration-related keywords
In [25]:
#Filtering the news (=rows) whose "title" use of the "migration_ita_regex" words 

migration_ita_regex = (
    r"\b(?:"
    #core migration terms
    r"immigrat\w*|emigrat\w*|migrant\w*|migratoria|immigrazione|emigrazione|migrazione|"      
    r"nato all['’]estero|nata all['’]estero|nati all['’]estero|nate all['’]estero|stranier\w*|"
    #legal status and protection
    r"rifugiat\w*|asilo politico|richiedente asilo|richiedenti asilo|protezione internazionale|"
    r"profug\w*|apolid\w*|senza patria|"
    #irregular migration
    r"trafficanti di esseri umani|trafficante di esseri umani|rimpatr\w*|espuls\w*|espellere|frontex|"
    #reception and asylum systems
    r"sistema d['’]asilo|sistema di asilo|procedura di asilo|procedura per l['’]asilo|"
    r"centri di permanenza per il rimpatrio|cpr|sistema di accoglienza e integrazione|sai|"
    r"centri di accoglienza straordinaria|cas|convenzione di dublino|accordi di dublino|regolamento di dublino|"
    #legal migration and permits
    r"permesso di soggiorno|permessi di soggiorno|visto da residente|visti da residente|"
    r"ricongiungimento familiare|ricongiungimenti familiari|residente permanente|residenti permanenti|"
    #citizenship debate
    r"cittadinanza|naturalizzazione|cittadin\w*|naturalizzat\w*|ius soli|ius sanguinis|"
    r"ius scholae|ius culturae|nazionalità|"
    #biggest migrants group
    r"rumeno|rumena|rumeni|rumene|"
    r"albanese|albanesi|"
    r"marocchino|marocchina|marocchini|marocchine|"
    r"cinese|cinesi|"
    r"ucraino|ucraina|ucraini|ucraine"
    r")\b"
)

# Filter the dataset by selecting articles whose title contains at least one migration-related keyword included in the dictionary.
# The search is: case-insensitive, regex-based, robust to missing values

df_imm = df_imm[
    df_imm["title"].str.contains(migration_ita_regex, case=False, na=False, regex=True) 
]
In [26]:
print("Number of remaining articles:", len(df_imm_2016))

In [27]:
# Removing potential sources of noise

migrants_ita_regex = (
    r"\b(?:"
    #biggest migrants group
    r"rumeno|rumena|rumeni|rumene|"
    r"albanese|albanesi|"
    r"marocchino|marocchina|marocchini|marocchine|"
    r"cinese|cinesi|"
    r"ucraino|ucraina|ucraini|ucraine"
    r")\b"
)

context_noise_regex = (
    r"\b(?:"
    # football/sport
    r"calciomercato|milan|inter|juventus|lazio|"
    r"serie\s*a|serie\s*b|champions|europa\s*league|"
    r"partita|gol|allenatore|calciatore|attaccante|difensore|"
    r"basket|tennis|motogp|formula\s*1|sport|calcio|olimpiadi|"

    # foreign policy and their leaders
    r"primo\s*ministro|presidente|premier|governo|parlamento|"
    r"ambasciatore|ministro\s+degli\s+esteri|"
    r"pechino|tirana|kiev|bucarest|rabat|"

    #economy
    r"mercato|economia|industria"
    r")\b"
)

mask_noise = (
    df_imm["title"].str.contains(migrants_ita_regex, case=False, na=False, regex=True) &
    df_imm["title"].str.contains(context_noise_regex, case=False, na=False, regex=True)
)

df_noise = df_imm[mask_noise]

pd.set_option("display.max_colwidth", None)
df_noise[["media_name", "publish_date", "title"]].head(50)
Out[27]:

In [28]:
df_imm_clean = df_imm[~mask_noise].copy()
In [29]:
print("Original sample:", len(df_imm))
print("Noise:", mask_noise.sum())
print("Final sample:", len(df_imm_clean))

Original sample: 11456
Noise: 279
Final sample: 11177

Removing duplicates¶

Remove duplicate news items based on article titles. Redundancy in the dataset is reduced by retaining only one observation for each unique title.

In [30]:
df_imm = df_imm_clean.drop_duplicates(subset="title")
print("Number of remaining articles:", len(df_imm))
df_imm["title"].head(20)

Number of remaining articles: 8203

Labeling welfare-related articles¶

Dictionary-based filtering of welfare-related news articles using a rule-based textual classification approach.

In [33]:
df_imm = pd.read_csv("ITA_clean.csv")
df_imm.head()
Out[33]:
id language media_name publish_date title full_2016 pre_interview post_interview media_categories
0 f02fca7636c09f9b899f7bd1a1d407741ca8c7fb97a90f0d27867eb03e55c533 it La Repubblica 2016-01-01 Merkel ai tedeschi: "Migranti sono un'opportunità". Hollande: "Non abbiamo finito con il terrorismo" 1 0 0 Quality Progressive
1 4b8ec84910c45842f0260e6f8c94be73a25d45ed2c28eb101cbb379e48547a2d it La Repubblica 2016-10-10 La cinese Wanda e il no di Ubs 1 0 0 Quality Progressive
2 79941485465069a7e620d42d0974caeb8b439ca18e9e9cbf6cdd8af22d4148b4 it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Terrorismo, il ministro Alfano: «Espulso algerino di Bergamo» 1 0 0 Quality Conservative
3 9b31f5cd1fc7247991a9d466bff990bd5906578eead0f71b894ded89302bb64c it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Manomorta in metrò: la ragazza cinese si arrabbia e volano gli schiaffi 1 0 0 Quality Conservative
4 d3e3b5085e3cea44c8415a3b3627825bec123181929d6fd28c22ada4ca8295b3 it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Terrorismo, Alfano: ''Espulso algerino. 57 da inizio 2016'' 1 0 0 Quality Conservative
In [34]:
# Create a dichotomous variable marking migration-related news items (is_imm)
# Note: all rows in the cleaned dataset are migration-related news

df_imm["is_imm"] = True
df_imm.head()
Out[34]:
id language media_name publish_date title full_2016 pre_interview post_interview media_categories is_imm
0 f02fca7636c09f9b899f7bd1a1d407741ca8c7fb97a90f0d27867eb03e55c533 it La Repubblica 2016-01-01 Merkel ai tedeschi: "Migranti sono un'opportunità". Hollande: "Non abbiamo finito con il terrorismo" 1 0 0 Quality Progressive True
1 4b8ec84910c45842f0260e6f8c94be73a25d45ed2c28eb101cbb379e48547a2d it La Repubblica 2016-10-10 La cinese Wanda e il no di Ubs 1 0 0 Quality Progressive True
2 79941485465069a7e620d42d0974caeb8b439ca18e9e9cbf6cdd8af22d4148b4 it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Terrorismo, il ministro Alfano: «Espulso algerino di Bergamo» 1 0 0 Quality Conservative True
3 9b31f5cd1fc7247991a9d466bff990bd5906578eead0f71b894ded89302bb64c it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Manomorta in metrò: la ragazza cinese si arrabbia e volano gli schiaffi 1 0 0 Quality Conservative True
4 d3e3b5085e3cea44c8415a3b3627825bec123181929d6fd28c22ada4ca8295b3 it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Terrorismo, Alfano: ''Espulso algerino. 57 da inizio 2016'' 1 0 0 Quality Conservative True

The welfare-related textual model is constructed relying on 3 complementary groups of regex patterns:

  1. wf_ita_direct_regex: includes terms that directly refer to explicitly welfare-related policy domains.
  2. wf_ita_conditional_regex: includes broader socio-economic and policy-related terms whose welfare relevance is context-dependent. These terms are classified as welfare-related only when co-occurring with words suggesting an intervention from the state.
  3. institutional_filter_regex: includes institutional and policy-oriented terms used to disambiguate context-dependent matches included in wf_ita_conditional_regex. Co-occurrence between the second and third regex groups is interpreted as evidence of welfare-related news coverage.

This co-occurrence logic reduces false positives and improves contextual validity in dictionary-based text classification.

In [35]:
wf_ita_direct_regex = (
    # core welfare terms
    r"welfare state|protezione sociale|sicurezza sociale|assistenza sociale|"
    r"assicurazione sociale|spesa sociale|welfare pubblico|riforma del welfare|"
    r"riforma della politica sociale|contribut\w*|contribuzion\w*|"
    # gender equality
    r"gender pay gap|divario retributivo di genere|congedo di maternità|"
    r"congedo di paternità|congedo parentale|"
    # wages
    r"salari\w*|paga|stipendi\w*|contrattazione collettiva|contratto collettivo| lavoro povero|"
    # information about employment conditions and protection in case of dismissals & unemployment benefits
    r"centro per l['’]impiego|centri per l['’]impiego|"
    r"condizioni di lavoro|cassa integrazione|fondo di garanzia|"
    r"tutela dell['’]occupazione|tutela del posto di lavoro|"
    r"servizio per l['’]impiego|servizi per l['’]impiego|"
    r"ufficio di collocamento|ufficio per l['’]impiego|licenziament\w*|licenziat\w*|"
    # social dialogue and involvement workers
    r"sindacat\w*|sindacal\w*|scioper\w*|dialogo sociale|relazioni industriali|rappresentanza dei lavoratori|"
    # healthy, safe and well-adapted work environemnt and data protection
    r"sicurezza sul lavoro|salute e sicurezza sul lavoro|sicurezza sul posto di lavoro|infortunio sul lavoro|infortuni sul lavoro|"
    r"malattia professionale|malattie professionali|"
    # childcare and support to children
    r"servizi per l['’]infanzia|prima infanzia|asilo nido|asili nido|nido d['’]infanzia|bambin\w*|figli\w*|famigl\w*|"
    # social protection & minimum income
    r"servizi sociali|lavoratori sociali|prestazione sociale|prestazioni sociali|pover\w*|"
    r"assistenza di comunità|servizi assistenziali|mense per i poveri|mensa per i poveri|"
    r"reddito minimo|reddito di base|reddito universale di base|reddito garantito|"
    r"schema di sostegno al reddito|reddito d['’]inclusione|REI|"
    # old-age income and pensions
    r"pension\w*|anzianità|invecchiamento|reddito in vecchiaia|"
    # health care
    r"sanità|assistenza sanitaria|sistema sanitario|servizio sanitario nazionale|SSN|"
    r"dottor\w*|spesa sanitaria|costi sanitari|lista d['’]attesa|liste d['’]attesa"
)

wf_ita_conditional_regex = (
    # education, training and life-long learning
    r"scuol\w*|apprendimento permanente|lifelong learning|insegnant\w*|docent\w*|"
    r"professor\w*|apprendistat\w*|tirocin\w*|istruzione professionale|"
    r"istruzione tecnica|istruzione superiore|lice\w*|istruzione universitaria|università|"
    r"occupabilit\w*|aggiornamento professionale|riqualificazion\w*|"
    r"integrazione nel mercato del lavoro|"
    # equal opportunities
    r"parità salariale|parità di retribuzione|retribuzione uguale|retribuzione diseguale|"
    r"trattamento diseguale|trattamento uguale|pari opportunità|differenti opportunità|"
    r"gruppo svantaggiato|gruppi svantaggiati|"
    # active support to employment
    r"attivazione|disoccupazion\w*|occupazion\w*|occupat\w*|disoccupat\w*|"
    r"senza lavoro|in cerca di lavoro|NEET|lavoro autonomo|"
    # secure and adaptable employment
    r"mercato del lavoro|contratto temporaneo|contratto a termine|"
    r"contratto a tempo determinato|contratto a tempo indeterminato|contratto atipico|"
    r"lavoro atipico|lavoratore atipico|lavoratore precario|lavoro precario|"
    r"lavoratori precari|lavoro informale|lavoro nero|lavoro non dichiarato|"
    r"lavoro su piattaforma|lavoro sulle piattaforme|platform work|flexicurity|flessicurezza|"
    # work-life balance
    r"responsabilità di cura|doveri di cura|responsabilità familiari|work-life balance|"
    r"conciliazione vita-lavoro|equilibrio vita-lavoro|lavoro part-time|"
    r"lavoro a tempo parziale|lavoro full-time|lavoro a tempo pieno|"
    r"orario flessibile|lavoro flessibile|congedo per malattia|"
    # inclusion of people with disabilities
    r"disabil\w*|handicap\w*|invalid\w*|accessibilità|"
    # long-term care
    r"assistenza a lungo termine|cure a lungo termine|assistenza domiciliare|"
    r"servizi di cura|casa di riposo|case di riposo|RSA|residenza sanitaria assistenziale|"
    # housing and assistance for homeless
    r"housing|alloggi\w*|abitazion\w*|politiche abitative|senza tetto|"
    r"senza fissa dimora|affitt\w*|centro di accoglienza|centri di accoglienza|"
    r"centro accoglienza|centri accoglienza"
)

institutional_filter_regex = (
    r"sussidi\w*|indennit\w*|sovvenzion\w*|trasferimento monetario|"
    r"trasferimenti monetari|politica|politiche|assicurazion\w*|riform\w*|"
    r"tagli alla spesa|taglio alla spesa|aumento della spesa|austerity|"
    r"politica di austerità|regolament\w*|contrast\w*|lotta|lotte"
)
In [36]:
# Create a textual series including all article titles
title = df_imm["title"].fillna("")

# Identify titles containing terms directly associated with welfare state policies and social protection domains.
direct_match = title.str.contains(
    wf_ita_direct_regex, case=False, regex=True, na=False)

# Identify titles containing context-dependent welfare-related terms.
conditional_match = title.str.contains(
    wf_ita_conditional_regex, case=False, regex=True, na=False)

# Identify titles containing institutional and policy-related terminology.
institutional_match = title.str.contains(
    institutional_filter_regex, case=False, regex=True, na=False )

# Create a binary indicator for welfare-related news coverage.
# Articles are classified as welfare-related if:
### 1. the title directly matches welfare-related keywords 
### 2. the title simultaneously contains:
#    - a conditional welfare-related term
#    - an institutional/policy-related term

df_imm["is_wf"] = direct_match | (conditional_match & institutional_match)
In [37]:
df_imm.head()
Out[37]:
id language media_name publish_date title full_2016 pre_interview post_interview media_categories is_imm is_wf

1 4b8ec84910c45842f0260e6f8c94be73a25d45ed2c28eb101cbb379e48547a2d it La Repubblica 2016-10-10 La cinese Wanda e il no di Ubs 1 0 0 Quality Progressive True False
2 79941485465069a7e620d42d0974caeb8b439ca18e9e9cbf6cdd8af22d4148b4 it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Terrorismo, il ministro Alfano: «Espulso algerino di Bergamo» 1 0 0 Quality Conservative True False
3 9b31f5cd1fc7247991a9d466bff990bd5906578eead0f71b894ded89302bb64c it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Manomorta in metrò: la ragazza cinese si arrabbia e volano gli schiaffi 1 0 0 Quality Conservative True False
4 d3e3b5085e3cea44c8415a3b3627825bec123181929d6fd28c22ada4ca8295b3 it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Terrorismo, Alfano: ''Espulso algerino. 57 da inizio 2016'' 1 0 0 Quality Conservative True False
In [38]:
# Dichotomous variable (Immigration, Welfare & Immigration)

def policy_domain(row):
    if row["is_imm"] == True and row["is_wf"] == True:
        return "Welfare & Immigration"
    elif row["is_imm"] == True and row["is_wf"] == False:
        return "Immigration"
    else:
        return "Other"

df_imm["domain"] = df_imm.apply(policy_domain, axis=1)
In [39]:
domain_counts = df_imm["domain"].value_counts()

total_articles = len(df_imm)

domain_percentages = (
    domain_counts / total_articles * 100
).round(2)

summary_table = pd.DataFrame({
    "count": domain_counts,
    "percentage": domain_percentages
})

print(summary_table)
print(f"-----------")
print(f"TOTAL COUNT OF ARTICLES:", len(df_imm))

                       count  percentage
domain                                  
Immigration             7734       94.28
Welfare & Immigration    469        5.72
-----------
TOTAL COUNT OF ARTICLES: 8203
In [40]:
pd.crosstab(
    df_imm["domain"],
    df_imm["media_name"],
    normalize="columns"
).round(3)

domain
Immigration 0.947 0.94 0.941

In [41]:
# From categorical to binary classification of the variable

def dicht_domain(row):
    if row["domain"] == "Immigration":
        return 0
    elif row["domain"] == "Welfare & Immigration":
        return 1
    else:
        return "Other"

df_imm["dicht_domain"] = df_imm.apply(dicht_domain, axis=1)
In [42]:
df_imm.head()

Exploratory data analysis of the Welfare & Immigration news sample¶

In [46]:
df_imm = pd.read_csv("ITA_label.csv")
df_imm.head()
Out[46]:
id language media_name publish_date title full_2016 pre_interview post_interview media_categories dicht_domain

In [47]:
df_imm_2016 = df_imm[df_imm["full_2016"] == 1].copy()
In [48]:
# From string to datetime
df_imm_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df_imm_2016["publish_date"]) 

# Daily counts of news per each outlets
domain_per_day_2016 = (
    df_imm_2016
    .groupby(
        [df_imm_2016["publish_date"].dt.date, "dicht_domain"]  #grouping news by dicht_domain per day
    )
    .size()                                             #counting the number of rows (=stories)
    .reset_index(name="count")                          #transformation into a normal DataFrame (count)
)

domain_per_day_2016["dicht_domain"] = (
    domain_per_day_2016["dicht_domain"]
    .replace({
        0: "Immigration",
        1: "Welfare & Immigration"
    })
)

print(domain_per_day_2016)

    publish_date           dicht_domain  count
0     2016-01-01            Immigration      3
1     2016-01-02            Immigration      6
2     2016-01-02  Welfare & Immigration      1
3     2016-01-03            Immigration      5
4     2016-01-04            Immigration     10
..           ...                    ...    ...
533   2016-12-29            Immigration     19
534   2016-12-29  Welfare & Immigration      2
535   2016-12-30            Immigration     11
536   2016-12-31            Immigration     22
537   2016-12-31  Welfare & Immigration      2

[538 rows x 3 columns]
In [49]:
# Graphical style
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

# Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(15,10))

sns.lineplot(
    data=domain_per_day_2016,
    x="publish_date",
    y="count",
    hue="dicht_domain",
    linewidth=2.5
)

# Better x axis fomatting (date formatting) 
ax = plt.gca() 

ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=2))
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(
    mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y")
)                                  #show the x-label every 2 months (shortened format)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)            #rotating the x-label of 45 degree

# Labels
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Articles per day")
plt.title("Migration-related coverage across Italian newspaper outlets")

# Legend
plt.legend(
    title="Policy domain",
    frameon=False,              #no border
    bbox_to_anchor=(1.02, 1),   #just outside the graph
    loc="upper left"
)

sns.despine()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [50]:
domain_outlet_per_day_2016 = (
    df_imm_2016
    .groupby([
        df_imm_2016["publish_date"].dt.date,
        "media_name",
        "media_categories",
        "dicht_domain"
    ])
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="count")
)

domain_outlet_per_day_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    domain_outlet_per_day_2016["publish_date"]
)

print(domain_outlet_per_day_2016)

     publish_date              media_name      media_categories  dicht_domain  \
0      2016-01-01  Il Corriere della Sera  Quality Conservative             0   
1      2016-01-01           La Repubblica   Quality Progressive             0   
2      2016-01-02  Il Corriere della Sera  Quality Conservative             0   
3      2016-01-02  Il Corriere della Sera  Quality Conservative             1   
4      2016-01-02           La Repubblica   Quality Progressive             0   
...           ...                     ...                   ...           ...   
1070   2016-12-30                  Libero               Popular             0   
1071   2016-12-31  Il Corriere della Sera  Quality Conservative             0   
1072   2016-12-31  Il Corriere della Sera  Quality Conservative             1   
1073   2016-12-31           La Repubblica   Quality Progressive             0   
1074   2016-12-31                  Libero               Popular             0   

      count  
0         1  
1         2  
2         4  
3         1  
4         2  
...     ...

[1075 rows x 5 columns]
In [51]:
# Ensure datetime
df_imm_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df_imm_2016["publish_date"])

# Rename binary domain labels
df_imm_2016["domain_label"] = df_imm_2016["dicht_domain"].replace({
    0: "Immigration only",
    1: "Welfare & Immigration"
})

# Count articles per day, outlet, and domain
domain_outlet_per_day_2016 = (
    df_imm_2016
    .groupby([
        df_imm_2016["publish_date"].dt.date,
        "media_name",
        "media_categories",
        "domain_label"
    ])
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="count")
)

# Plot
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

g = sns.relplot(
    data=domain_outlet_per_day_2016,
    x="publish_date",
    y="count",
    hue="media_name",
    col="domain_label",
    kind="line",
    linewidth=2.5,
    height=5,
    aspect=1.5,
    facet_kws={"sharey": False}
)

# Format axes
for ax in g.axes.flat:
    ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=2))
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y"))
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

g.set_axis_labels("Date", "Articles per day")
g.set_titles("{col_name}")

g._legend.set_title("Outlet")
g._legend.set_bbox_to_anchor((1.05, 0.5))
g._legend._loc = 6   # center left

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [52]:
df_imm_pre = df_imm[df_imm["pre_interview"] == 1].copy()

    .reset_index(name="count")                          #transformation into a normal DataFrame (count)
)

domain_per_day_pre["dicht_domain"] = (
    domain_per_day_pre["dicht_domain"]

        0: "Immigration",
        1: "Welfare & Immigration"
    })
)

In [56]:
# Ensure datetime
df_imm_pre["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df_imm_pre["publish_date"])

# Rename binary domain labels
df_imm_pre["domain_label"] = df_imm_pre["dicht_domain"].replace({
    0: "Immigration only",
    1: "Welfare & Immigration"
})

# Count articles per day, outlet, and domain
domain_outlet_per_day_pre = (
    df_imm_pre
    .groupby([
        df_imm_pre["publish_date"].dt.date,
        "media_name",
        "media_categories",
        "domain_label"
    ])
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="count")
)

# Plot
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

g = sns.relplot(
    data=domain_outlet_per_day_pre,
    x="publish_date",
    y="count",
    hue="media_name",
    col="domain_label",
    kind="line",
    linewidth=2.5,
    height=5,
    aspect=1.5,
    facet_kws={"sharey": False}
)

# Format axes
for ax in g.axes.flat:
    ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=2))
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y"))
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

g.set_axis_labels("Date", "Articles per day")
g.set_titles("{col_name}")

g._legend.set_title("Outlet")
g._legend.set_bbox_to_anchor((1.05, 0.5))
g._legend._loc = 6   # center left

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

    )

    .reset_index(name="count")                          #transformation into a normal DataFrame (count)
)

domain_per_day_post["dicht_domain"] = (
    domain_per_day_post["dicht_domain"]
    .replace({
        0: "Immigration",
        1: "Welfare & Immigration"

)

In [61]:
# Ensure datetime
df_imm_post["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df_imm_post["publish_date"])

# Rename binary domain labels
df_imm_post["domain_label"] = df_imm_post["dicht_domain"].replace({
    0: "Immigration only",
    1: "Welfare & Immigration"
})

# Count articles per day, outlet, and domain
domain_outlet_per_day_post = (
    df_imm_post
    .groupby([
        df_imm_post["publish_date"].dt.date,
        "media_name",
        "domain_label"
    ])
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="count")
)

# Plot
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

g = sns.relplot(
    data=domain_outlet_per_day_post,
    x="publish_date",
    y="count",
    hue="media_name",
    col="domain_label",
    kind="line",
    linewidth=2.5,
    height=5,
    aspect=1.5,
    facet_kws={"sharey": False}
)

# Format axes
for ax in g.axes.flat:
    ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=2))
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y"))
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

g.set_axis_labels("Date", "Articles per day")
g.set_titles("{col_name}")

g._legend.set_title("Outlet")
g._legend.set_bbox_to_anchor((1.05, 0.5))
g._legend._loc = 6   # center left

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

(a) Full 2016¶

In [62]:
# Ensure datetime
domain_per_day_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    domain_per_day_2016["publish_date"]
)

# Sort values
domain_per_day_2016 = domain_per_day_2016.sort_values(
    ["dicht_domain", "publish_date"]
)

# 7-day rolling average
domain_per_day_2016["rolling_7d"] = (
    domain_per_day_2016
    .groupby("dicht_domain")["count"]
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(7, min_periods=1).mean())
)

# Graphical style
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

# Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(15,10))

sns.lineplot(
    data=domain_per_day_2016,
    x="publish_date",
    y="rolling_7d",
    hue="dicht_domain",
    linewidth=2.5
)

# Better x axis formatting
ax = plt.gca()

ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=2))
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(
    mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y")
)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)

# Labels
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Articles per day (7-day rolling average)")
plt.title("Migration-related coverage by policy domain")

# Legend
plt.legend(
    title="Policy domain",
    frameon=False,
    bbox_to_anchor=(1.02, 1),
    loc="upper left"
)

sns.despine()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [63]:
# Ensure datetime
domain_outlet_per_day_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    domain_outlet_per_day_2016["publish_date"]
)

# Sort values
domain_outlet_per_day_2016 = domain_outlet_per_day_2016.sort_values(
    ["media_name", "domain_label", "publish_date"]
)

# 30-day rolling average
domain_outlet_per_day_2016["rolling_30d"] = (
    domain_outlet_per_day_2016
    .groupby(["media_name", "domain_label"])["count"]
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(30, min_periods=1).mean())
)

# 7-day rolling average
domain_outlet_per_day_2016["rolling_7d"] = (
    domain_outlet_per_day_2016
    .groupby(["media_name", "domain_label"])["count"]
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(7, min_periods=1).mean())
)


# Plot style
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

g = sns.relplot(
    data=domain_outlet_per_day_2016,
    x="publish_date",
    y="rolling_30d",
    hue="media_name",
    col="domain_label",
    kind="line",
    linewidth=2.5,
    height=5,
    aspect=1.5,
    facet_kws={"sharey": False}
)

for ax in g.axes.flat:
    ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=2))
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y"))
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

g.set_axis_labels(
    "Date",
    "Articles per day (30-day rolling average)"
)

g.set_titles("{col_name}")
g._legend.set_title("Outlet")
g._legend.set_bbox_to_anchor((1.05, 0.5))
g._legend._loc = 6   # center left

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [64]:
domain_outlet_per_day_2016.head()
Out[64]:
publish_date media_name media_categories domain_label count rolling_30d rolling_7d
0 2016-01-01 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative Immigration only 1 1.000000 1.000000
2 2016-01-02 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative Immigration only 4 2.500000 2.500000
5 2016-01-03 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative Immigration only 2 2.333333 2.333333
7 2016-01-04 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative Immigration only 7 3.500000 3.500000
9 2016-01-05 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative Immigration only 5 3.800000 3.800000

 (b) Pre-interview¶

In [65]:
# Ensure datetime
domain_per_day_pre["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    domain_per_day_pre["publish_date"]
)

# Sort values
domain_per_day_pre = domain_per_day_pre.sort_values(
    ["dicht_domain", "publish_date"]
)

# 7-day rolling average
domain_per_day_pre["rolling_7d"] = (
    domain_per_day_pre
    .groupby("dicht_domain")["count"]
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(7, min_periods=1).mean())
)

# Graphical style
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

# Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(15,10))

sns.lineplot(
    data=domain_per_day_pre,
    x="publish_date",
    y="rolling_7d",
    hue="dicht_domain",
    linewidth=2.5
)

# Better x axis formatting
ax = plt.gca()

ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=1))
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(
    mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y")
)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)

# Labels
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Articles per day (7-day rolling average)")
plt.title("Migration-related coverage by policy domain")

# Legend
plt.legend(
    title="Policy domain",
    frameon=False,
    bbox_to_anchor=(1.02, 1),
    loc="upper left"
)

sns.despine()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [66]:
# Ensure datetime
domain_outlet_per_day_pre["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    domain_outlet_per_day_pre["publish_date"]
)

# Sort values
domain_outlet_per_day_pre = domain_outlet_per_day_pre.sort_values(
    ["media_name", "domain_label", "publish_date"]
)

# 30-day rolling average
domain_outlet_per_day_pre["rolling_30d"] = (
    domain_outlet_per_day_pre
    .groupby(["media_name", "domain_label"])["count"]
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(30, min_periods=1).mean())
)

# 7-day rolling average
domain_outlet_per_day_pre["rolling_7d"] = (
    domain_outlet_per_day_pre
    .groupby(["media_name", "domain_label"])["count"]
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(7, min_periods=1).mean())
)


# Plot style
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

g = sns.relplot(
    data=domain_outlet_per_day_pre,
    x="publish_date",
    y="rolling_30d",
    hue="media_name",
    col="domain_label",
    kind="line",
    linewidth=2.5,
    height=5,
    aspect=1.5,
    facet_kws={"sharey": False}
)

for ax in g.axes.flat:
    ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=1))
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y"))
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

g.set_axis_labels(
    "Date",
    "Articles per day (30-day rolling average)"
)

g.set_titles("{col_name}")
g._legend.set_title("Outlet")
g._legend.set_bbox_to_anchor((1.05, 0.5))
g._legend._loc = 6   # center left

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [67]:
domain_outlet_per_day_pre.head()
Out[67]:
publish_date media_name media_categories domain_label count rolling_30d rolling_7d
0 2017-06-11 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative Immigration only 1 1.000000 1.000000
3 2017-06-13 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative Immigration only 6 3.500000 3.500000
6 2017-06-14 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative Immigration only 7 4.666667 4.666667
9 2017-06-15 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative Immigration only 7 5.250000 5.250000
13 2017-06-16 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative Immigration only 3 4.800000 4.800000

(c) Post-interview¶

In [68]:
# Ensure datetime
domain_per_day_post["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    domain_per_day_post["publish_date"]
)

# Sort values
domain_per_day_post = domain_per_day_post.sort_values(
    ["dicht_domain", "publish_date"]
)

# 7-day rolling average
domain_per_day_post["rolling_7d"] = (
    domain_per_day_post
    .groupby("dicht_domain")["count"]
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(7, min_periods=1).mean())
)

# Graphical style
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

# Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(15,10))

sns.lineplot(
    data=domain_per_day_post,
    x="publish_date",
    y="rolling_7d",
    hue="dicht_domain",
    linewidth=2.5
)

# Better x axis formatting
ax = plt.gca()

ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=1))
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(
    mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y")
)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)

# Labels
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Articles per day (7-day rolling average)")
plt.title("Migration-related coverage by policy domain")

# Legend
plt.legend(
    title="Policy domain",
    frameon=False,
    bbox_to_anchor=(1.02, 1),
    loc="upper left"
)

sns.despine()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [69]:
# Ensure datetime
domain_outlet_per_day_post["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    domain_outlet_per_day_post["publish_date"]
)

# Sort values
domain_outlet_per_day_post = domain_outlet_per_day_post.sort_values(
    ["media_name", "domain_label", "publish_date"]
)

# 30-day rolling average
domain_outlet_per_day_post["rolling_30d"] = (
    domain_outlet_per_day_post
    .groupby(["media_name", "domain_label"])["count"]
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(30, min_periods=1).mean())
)

# 7-day rolling average
domain_outlet_per_day_post["rolling_7d"] = (
    domain_outlet_per_day_post
    .groupby(["media_name", "domain_label"])["count"]
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(7, min_periods=1).mean())
)


# Plot style
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

g = sns.relplot(
    data=domain_outlet_per_day_post,
    x="publish_date",
    y="rolling_30d",
    hue="media_name",
    col="domain_label",
    kind="line",
    linewidth=2.5,
    height=5,
    aspect=1.5,
    facet_kws={"sharey": False}
)

for ax in g.axes.flat:
    ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=1))
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y"))
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

g.set_axis_labels(
    "Date",
    "Articles per day (30-day rolling average)"
)

g.set_titles("{col_name}")
g._legend.set_title("Outlet")
g._legend.set_bbox_to_anchor((1.05, 0.5))
g._legend._loc = 6   # center left

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

#News outlet categories
outlet_categories = {
    "La Repubblica": "Quality Progressive",
    "Il Corriere della Sera": "Quality Conservative",
    "Libero": "Popular"
}

domain_outlet_per_day_pre["media_categories"] = (
    domain_outlet_per_day_pre["media_name"].map(outlet_categories)
)
In [74]:
#Grouping news per month

domain_outlet_per_day_pre["month"] = domain_outlet_per_day_pre["publish_date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)
domain_outlet_per_day_pre.head()
Out[74]:
publish_date media_name media_categories domain_label count rolling_30d rolling_7d cntry month

In [75]:
#Overall-country level

domain_country_per_month_pre = (
    domain_outlet_per_day_pre
    .groupby(["cntry", "month", "domain_label"], as_index=False)
    ["count"].sum()
    .rename(columns={"count": "monthly_count_country"})
    .pivot_table(
        index=["cntry", "month"],
        columns="domain_label",
        values="monthly_count_country",
        fill_value=0
    )
    .reset_index()
)

domain_country_per_month_pre.head()
Out[75]:
domain_label cntry month Immigration only Welfare & Immigration
0 IT 2017-06 332.0 21.0
1 IT 2017-07 451.0 29.0
2 IT 2017-08 412.0 26.0
3 IT 2017-09 302.0 24.0
4 IT 2017-10 300.0 34.0
In [76]:
domain_outlet_per_month_pre = (
    domain_outlet_per_day_pre
    .groupby(["cntry", "media_categories", "media_name", "month", "domain_label"], as_index=False)
    ["count"].sum()
    .rename(columns={"count": "monthly_count_outlet"})
    .pivot_table(
        index=["cntry", "month", "media_name", "media_categories"],
        columns="domain_label",
        values="monthly_count_outlet",
        fill_value=0
    )
    .reset_index()
)
domain_outlet_per_month_pre.head()
Out[76]:
domain_label cntry month media_name media_categories Immigration only Welfare & Immigration

1 IT 2017-06 La Repubblica Quality Progressive 159.0 10.0
2 IT 2017-06 Libero Popular 102.0 5.0
3 IT 2017-07 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative 104.0 7.0
4 IT 2017-07 La Repubblica Quality Progressive 202.0 10.0
In [77]:
domain_country_per_month_pre["media_name"] = "TOTAL"

domain_month_pre = pd.concat(
    [
        domain_country_per_month_pre,
        domain_outlet_per_month_pre
    ],
    ignore_index=True
)
domain_month_pre.head()
Out[77]:
domain_label cntry month Immigration only Welfare & Immigration media_name media_categories
0 IT 2017-06 332.0 21.0 TOTAL NaN
1 IT 2017-07 451.0 29.0 TOTAL NaN
2 IT 2017-08 412.0 26.0 TOTAL NaN
3 IT 2017-09 302.0 24.0 TOTAL NaN
4 IT 2017-10 300.0 34.0 TOTAL NaN
In [78]:
country_monthly_total = pd.read_csv("ITA_country_month.csv")
media_monthly_total = pd.read_csv("ITA_media_month.csv")
In [79]:
#Relative number per media source

media_attention_monthly = domain_month_pre.merge(
    media_monthly_total,
    on=["media_name", "month"],
    how="left"
)

media_attention_monthly["media_attention_imm"] = (
    media_attention_monthly["Immigration only"] /
    media_attention_monthly["media_total_stories_month"]
)

media_attention_monthly["media_attention_imm_wf"] = (
    media_attention_monthly["Welfare & Immigration"] /
    media_attention_monthly["media_total_stories_month"]
)

media_attention_monthly["media_imm_prc"] = (
    media_attention_monthly["media_attention_imm"] * 100
)

media_attention_monthly["media_imm_wf_prc"] = (
    media_attention_monthly["media_attention_imm_wf"] * 100
)

#Relative number in the country

domain_month_pre = media_attention_monthly.merge(
    country_monthly_total,
    on=["month"],
    how="left"
)

domain_month_pre["country_attention_imm"] = (
    domain_month_pre["Immigration only"] /
    domain_month_pre["country_total_stories_month"]
)

domain_month_pre["country_attention_imm_wf"] = (
    domain_month_pre["Welfare & Immigration"] /
    domain_month_pre["country_total_stories_month"]
)

domain_month_pre["country_imm_prc"] = (
    domain_month_pre["country_attention_imm"] * 100
)

domain_month_pre["country_imm_wf_prc"] = (
    domain_month_pre["country_attention_imm_wf"] * 100
)
In [80]:
domain_month_pre.drop(columns=["media_total_stories_month", "media_attention_imm", "media_attention_imm_wf", 
                                "country_total_stories_month", "country_attention_imm", "country_attention_imm_wf", 
                                "source_id"], inplace=True)

domain_month_pre = domain_month_pre[
    [
        "cntry", 
        "month",
        "media_categories",
        "media_name",
        "Immigration only",
        "media_imm_prc",
        "country_imm_prc",
        "Welfare & Immigration",
        "media_imm_wf_prc",
        "country_imm_wf_prc"
    ]
]
domain_month_pre.head()

domain_month_pre.head()

#Relative number per media source

    media_monthly_total,
    on=["media_name", "month"],
    how="left"
)

media_attention_monthly["media_attention_imm"] = (
    media_attention_monthly["Immigration only"] /
    media_attention_monthly["media_total_stories_month"]
)

media_attention_monthly["media_attention_imm_wf"] = (
    media_attention_monthly["Welfare & Immigration"] /
    media_attention_monthly["media_total_stories_month"]
)

media_attention_monthly["media_imm_prc"] = (
    media_attention_monthly["media_attention_imm"] * 100
)

media_attention_monthly["media_imm_wf_prc"] = (
    media_attention_monthly["media_attention_imm_wf"] * 100
)

#Relative number in the country

domain_month_post = media_attention_monthly.merge(
    country_monthly_total,
    on=["month"],
    how="left"
)

domain_month_post["country_attention_imm"] = (
    domain_month_post["Immigration only"] /
    domain_month_post["country_total_stories_month"]
)

domain_month_post["country_attention_imm_wf"] = (
    domain_month_post["Welfare & Immigration"] /
    domain_month_post["country_total_stories_month"]
)

domain_month_post["country_imm_prc"] = (
    domain_month_post["country_attention_imm"] * 100
)

domain_month_post["country_imm_wf_prc"] = (
    domain_month_post["country_attention_imm_wf"] * 100
)

In [89]:
# Saving the dataset with counts

########### Pre-interviewing sample
output_dir = Path("/Users/saraeva/Nextcloud/TESI/DATA COLLECTION/CONTEXTUAL VARS/LONGITUDINAL")
output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

output_file = output_dir / "IT_pre_groups.csv"

domain_month_pre.to_csv(output_file, index=False, encoding= "utf-8")

########### Post-interviewing sample
output_dir = Path("/Users/saraeva/Nextcloud/TESI/DATA COLLECTION/CONTEXTUAL VARS/LONGITUDINAL")
output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

output_file = output_dir / "IT_post_groups.csv"

domain_month_post.to_csv(output_file, index=False, encoding= "utf-8")

Contextual-level dataset¶

In [90]:
df_imm = pd.read_csv("ITA_label.csv")
df_imm.head()
Out[90]:
id language media_name publish_date title full_2016 pre_interview post_interview media_categories dicht_domain
0 f02fca7636c09f9b899f7bd1a1d407741ca8c7fb97a90f0d27867eb03e55c533 it La Repubblica 2016-01-01 Merkel ai tedeschi: "Migranti sono un'opportunità". Hollande: "Non abbiamo finito con il terrorismo" 1 0 0 Quality Progressive 0
1 4b8ec84910c45842f0260e6f8c94be73a25d45ed2c28eb101cbb379e48547a2d it La Repubblica 2016-10-10 La cinese Wanda e il no di Ubs 1 0 0 Quality Progressive 0
2 79941485465069a7e620d42d0974caeb8b439ca18e9e9cbf6cdd8af22d4148b4 it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Terrorismo, il ministro Alfano: «Espulso algerino di Bergamo» 1 0 0 Quality Conservative 0
3 9b31f5cd1fc7247991a9d466bff990bd5906578eead0f71b894ded89302bb64c it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Manomorta in metrò: la ragazza cinese si arrabbia e volano gli schiaffi 1 0 0 Quality Conservative 0
4 d3e3b5085e3cea44c8415a3b3627825bec123181929d6fd28c22ada4ca8295b3 it Il Corriere della Sera 2016-10-11 Terrorismo, Alfano: ''Espulso algerino. 57 da inizio 2016'' 1 0 0 Quality Conservative 0
In [91]:
#News outlet categories
outlet_categories = {
    "La Repubblica": "Quality Progressive",
    "Il Corriere della Sera": "Quality Conservative",
    "Libero": "Popular"
}

df_imm["media_categories"] = (
    df_imm["media_name"].map(outlet_categories)
)
In [92]:
df_imm = df_imm[df_imm["full_2016"] == 1].copy()
df_imm["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df_imm["publish_date"])
df_imm["year"] = df_imm["publish_date"].dt.year

media_country_dataset = (
    df_imm
    .groupby(["year", "media_name", "media_categories"])
    .agg(
        n_imm_media_year=("id", "count"),
        n_imm_wf_media_year=("dicht_domain", "sum")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

country_totals = (
    df_imm
    .groupby("year")
    .agg(
        n_imm_country=("id", "count"),
        n_imm_wf_country=("dicht_domain", "sum")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

media_country_dataset = media_country_dataset.merge(
    country_totals,
    on="year",
    how="left"
)
In [93]:
media_country_dataset.head()
Out[93]:
year media_name media_categories n_imm_media_year n_imm_wf_media_year n_imm_country n_imm_wf_country
0 2016 Il Corriere della Sera Quality Conservative 2287 109 5046 262
1 2016 La Repubblica Quality Progressive 2032 120 5046 262
2 2016 Libero Popular 727 33 5046 262
In [94]:
country_yearly_total = pd.read_csv("ITA_country_total.csv")
media_attention_yearly = pd.read_csv("ITA_media_total.csv")
In [95]:
#Relative number per media source

media_attention_yearly = media_country_dataset.merge(
    media_attention_yearly,
    on=["media_name", "year"],
    how="left"
)

media_attention_yearly["media_attention_imm"] = (
    media_attention_yearly["n_imm_media_year"] /
    media_attention_yearly["media_total_stories_2016"]
)

media_attention_yearly["media_attention_imm_wf"] = (
    media_attention_yearly["n_imm_wf_media_year"] /
    media_attention_yearly["media_total_stories_2016"]
)

media_attention_yearly["media_imm_prc"] = (
    media_attention_yearly["media_attention_imm"] * 100
)

media_attention_yearly["media_imm_wf_prc"] = (
    media_attention_yearly["media_attention_imm_wf"] * 100
)

#Relative number in the country

media_country_dataset = media_attention_yearly.merge(
    country_yearly_total,
    on=["year"],
    how="left"
)

media_country_dataset["country_attention_imm"] = (
    media_country_dataset["n_imm_country"] /
    media_country_dataset["country_total_stories_2016"]
)

media_country_dataset["country_attention_imm_wf"] = (
    media_country_dataset["n_imm_wf_country"] /
    media_country_dataset["country_total_stories_2016"]
)

media_country_dataset["country_imm_prc"] = (
    media_country_dataset["country_attention_imm"] * 100
)

media_country_dataset["country_imm_wf_prc"] = (
    media_country_dataset["country_attention_imm_wf"] * 100
)
In [96]:
media_country_dataset.drop(columns=["media_total_stories_2016", "media_attention_imm", "media_attention_imm_wf", 
                                    "country_total_stories_2016", "country_attention_imm", "country_attention_imm_wf", 
                                    "source_id"], inplace=True)
media_country_dataset

In [99]:
# Saving the dataset with counts

output_dir = Path("/Users/saraeva/Nextcloud/TESI/DATA COLLECTION/CONTEXTUAL VARS/2016")
output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

output_file = output_dir / "IT_overall_2016.csv"

media_country_dataset.to_csv(output_file, index=False, encoding= "utf-8")

Visualization¶

In [100]:
df_imm_2016 = pd.read_csv("ITA_label.csv")
df_imm_2016 = df_imm_2016[df_imm_2016["full_2016"] == 1]
In [101]:
# Ensure datetime
df_imm_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df_imm_2016["publish_date"])
denominator_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(denominator_2016["publish_date"])

# Keep only Welfare & Immigration articles
df_wf_imm_2016 = df_imm_2016[
    df_imm_2016["dicht_domain"] == 1
].copy()

# Numerator: Welfare & Immigration articles per day
wf_imm_per_day_2016 = (
    df_wf_imm_2016
    .groupby(df_wf_imm_2016["publish_date"].dt.date)
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="wf_imm_count")
)

wf_imm_per_day_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    wf_imm_per_day_2016["publish_date"]
)

# Denominator: all stories per day
total_per_day_2016 = (
    denominator_2016
    .groupby(denominator["publish_date"].dt.date, as_index=False)
    .agg(total_stories=("total_stories", "sum"))
)

total_per_day_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    total_per_day_2016["publish_date"]
)

# Merge numerator and denominator
attention_daily = total_per_day_2016.merge(
    wf_imm_per_day_2016,
    on="publish_date",
    how="left"
)

attention_daily["wf_imm_count"] = attention_daily["wf_imm_count"].fillna(0)

# Relative attention per day
attention_daily["relative_attention"] = (
    attention_daily["wf_imm_count"] /
    attention_daily["total_stories"]
)

# Month
attention_daily["month"] = attention_daily["publish_date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

# Monthly average of relative attention
attention_monthly = (
    attention_daily
    .groupby("month", as_index=False)
    .agg(monthly_avg_relative_attention=("relative_attention", "mean"))
)

# Optional: percentage
attention_monthly["monthly_avg_relative_attention_prc"] = (
    attention_monthly["monthly_avg_relative_attention"] * 100
)

sns.set_style("whitegrid")

plt.figure(figsize=(14, 7))

ax = sns.lineplot(
    data=attention_monthly,
    x="month",
    y="monthly_avg_relative_attention_prc",
    marker="o",
    linewidth=2.5,
    color="darkorange"
)

ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=1))
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y"))
ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("Monthly average relative attention (%)")
ax.set_title("Monthly average relative attention to Welfare & Immigration")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [102]:
# Ensure datetime
df_imm_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df_imm_2016["publish_date"])
denominator_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(denominator_2016["publish_date"])

# Keep only Welfare & Immigration articles
df_wf_imm_2016 = df_imm_2016[
    df_imm_2016["dicht_domain"] == 1
].copy()

# Numerator: Welfare & Immigration articles per day and media source
wf_imm_per_day_media = (
    df_wf_imm_2016
    .groupby([
        "media_name",
        df_wf_imm_2016["publish_date"].dt.date
    ])
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="wf_imm_count")
)

wf_imm_per_day_media["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    wf_imm_per_day_media["publish_date"]
)

# Denominator: all stories per day and media source
total_per_day_media = (
    denominator_2016
    .groupby([
        "media_name",
        denominator_2016["publish_date"].dt.date
    ], as_index=False)
    .agg(total_stories=("total_stories", "sum"))
)

total_per_day_media["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    total_per_day_media["publish_date"]
)

# Merge numerator and denominator
attention_daily_media = total_per_day_media.merge(
    wf_imm_per_day_media,
    on=["media_name", "publish_date"],
    how="left"
)

attention_daily_media["wf_imm_count"] = (
    attention_daily_media["wf_imm_count"].fillna(0)
)

# Relative attention per day and media source
attention_daily_media["relative_attention"] = (
    attention_daily_media["wf_imm_count"] /
    attention_daily_media["total_stories"]
)

# Month
attention_daily_media["month"] = (
    attention_daily_media["publish_date"]
    .dt.to_period("M")
    .dt.to_timestamp()
)

# Monthly average relative attention per media source
attention_monthly_media = (
    attention_daily_media
    .groupby(["media_name", "month"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        monthly_avg_relative_attention=("relative_attention", "mean")
    )
)

attention_monthly_media["monthly_avg_relative_attention_prc"] = (
    attention_monthly_media["monthly_avg_relative_attention"] * 100
)

sns.set_style("whitegrid")

plt.figure(figsize=(14, 7))

ax = sns.lineplot(
    data=attention_monthly_media,
    x="month",
    y="monthly_avg_relative_attention_prc",
    hue="media_name",
    marker="o",
    linewidth=2
)

ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=1))
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y"))
ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("Monthly average relative attention (%)")
ax.set_title("Monthly average relative attention to Welfare & Immigration by media source")

ax.legend(
    title="Media source",
    bbox_to_anchor=(1.02, 1),
    loc="upper left"
)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [103]:
# Keep only Immigration articles
df_imm_2016 = df_imm_2016[
    df_imm_2016["dicht_domain"] == 0
].copy()

# Numerator: Welfare & Immigration articles per day
imm_per_day_2016 = (
    df_imm_2016
    .groupby(df_imm_2016["publish_date"].dt.date)
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="imm_count")
)

imm_per_day_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    imm_per_day_2016["publish_date"]
)

# Denominator: all stories per day
total_per_day_2016 = (
    denominator_2016
    .groupby(denominator_2016["publish_date"].dt.date, as_index=False)
    .agg(total_stories=("total_stories", "sum"))
)

total_per_day_2016["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    total_per_day_2016["publish_date"]
)

# Merge numerator and denominator
attention_daily = total_per_day_2016.merge(
    imm_per_day_2016,
    on="publish_date",
    how="left"
)

attention_daily["imm_count"] = attention_daily["imm_count"].fillna(0)

# Relative attention per day
attention_daily["relative_attention"] = (
    attention_daily["imm_count"] /
    attention_daily["total_stories"]
)

# Month
attention_daily["month"] = attention_daily["publish_date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

# Monthly average of relative attention
attention_monthly = (
    attention_daily
    .groupby("month", as_index=False)
    .agg(monthly_avg_relative_attention=("relative_attention", "mean"))
)

# Optional: percentage
attention_monthly["monthly_avg_relative_attention_prc"] = (
    attention_monthly["monthly_avg_relative_attention"] * 100
)

sns.set_style("whitegrid")

plt.figure(figsize=(14, 7))

ax = sns.lineplot(
    data=attention_monthly,
    x="month",
    y="monthly_avg_relative_attention_prc",
    marker="o",
    linewidth=2.5,
    color="darkorange"
)

ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=1))
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y"))
ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("Monthly average relative attention (%)")
ax.set_title("Monthly average relative attention to Immigration only")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [104]:
# Keep only Immigration articles
df_imm_2016 = df_imm_2016[
    df_imm_2016["dicht_domain"] == 0
].copy()

# Numerator: Welfare & Immigration articles per day and media source
imm_per_day_media = (
    df_imm_2016
    .groupby([
        "media_name",
        df_imm_2016["publish_date"].dt.date
    ])
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="wf_imm_count")
)

imm_per_day_media["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    imm_per_day_media["publish_date"]
)

# Denominator: all stories per day and media source
total_per_day_media = (
    denominator_2016
    .groupby([
        "media_name",
        denominator_2016["publish_date"].dt.date
    ], as_index=False)
    .agg(total_stories=("total_stories", "sum"))
)

total_per_day_media["publish_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    total_per_day_media["publish_date"]
)

# Merge numerator and denominator
attention_daily_media = total_per_day_media.merge(
    imm_per_day_media,
    on=["media_name", "publish_date"],
    how="left"
)

attention_daily_media["wf_imm_count"] = (
    attention_daily_media["wf_imm_count"].fillna(0)
)

# Relative attention per day and media source
attention_daily_media["relative_attention"] = (
    attention_daily_media["wf_imm_count"] /
    attention_daily_media["total_stories"]
)

# Month
attention_daily_media["month"] = (
    attention_daily_media["publish_date"]
    .dt.to_period("M")
    .dt.to_timestamp()
)

# Monthly average relative attention per media source
attention_monthly_media = (
    attention_daily_media
    .groupby(["media_name", "month"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        monthly_avg_relative_attention=("relative_attention", "mean")
    )
)

attention_monthly_media["monthly_avg_relative_attention_prc"] = (
    attention_monthly_media["monthly_avg_relative_attention"] * 100
)

sns.set_style("whitegrid")

plt.figure(figsize=(14, 7))

ax = sns.lineplot(
    data=attention_monthly_media,
    x="month",
    y="monthly_avg_relative_attention_prc",
    hue="media_name",
    marker="o",
    linewidth=2
)

ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=1))
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b %Y"))
ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("Monthly average relative attention (%)")
ax.set_title("Monthly average relative attention to Immigration only by media source")

ax.legend(
    title="Media source",
    bbox_to_anchor=(1.02, 1),
    loc="upper left"
)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
